info.name: User Auth Service
info.description:
Complete API collection for the user-auth-service microservice.

## Setup
1. Import this collection into Postman.
2. Set the collection variable `baseUrl` to your service URL (default: `http://localhost:4002`).
3. Set `tenantId` to any valid tenant slug (e.g. `tenant-a`).
4. Run **Register** or **Login** â€” the test scripts will auto-populate `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, and `userId`.
5. All protected requests automatically use `{{accessToken}}`.

## Variable Reference
| Variable | Auto-set by | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `baseUrl` | Manual | Service base URL |
| `tenantId` | Manual | Tenant slug (3â€“64 chars, lowercase) |
| `accessToken` | Login / Refresh | JWT access token |
| `refreshToken` | Login / Refresh | JWT refresh token |
| `userId` | Register / Login | Current user's `_id` |
| `sessionId` | Get Sessions | First active session ID |
| `deviceId` | Get Devices | First trusted device ID |
| `adminAccessToken` | Admin Login | Admin bearer token |
| `resetToken` | Manual | Password reset token (from email) |
| `verifyToken` | Manual | Email verification token (from email) |
| `mfaToken` | Login (MFA) | Short-lived MFA challenge token |
| `totpSecret` | Setup TOTP | TOTP base32 secret |

item.name: Health
item.description: Health check endpoints. These do NOT require X-Tenant-Id.

request.description:
Returns service status, version, uptime, and dependency health.

**Expected response:**
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "service": "user-auth-service",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uptime": 42
}
```
Kubernetes liveness probe. Always returns 200 if the process is running â€” no DB check.
Kubernetes readiness probe. Returns 200 only when MongoDB (and Redis if configured) is reachable. Returns 503 otherwise.

item.name: Auth â€” Public
item.description: Registration, login, password reset, email verification, OTP flows. All require `X-Tenant-Id` header.

request.description:
Register a new user in the tenant. Sends a verification email via the external email service.

**Password rules:** min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 digit, 1 special char.

**Returns:** `201 Created` with `{ user: { id, email, username, status } }`

Authenticate with email + password.

**Success:** Returns `accessToken`. Refresh token and ID token are set as HttpOnly cookies.

**MFA enabled:** Returns `{ mfaRequired: true, mfaToken }` â€” complete login via `POST /login/mfa`.

**Lockout:** After `MAX_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS` failures the account is locked and `ACCOUNT_LOCKED` email is sent.

Complete a two-step MFA login. Use the `mfaToken` received from `POST /login` and supply the TOTP code or OTP code.

On success returns `accessToken` (same as regular login).

Invalidate the current session. The access token JTI is added to the blacklist and all auth cookies are cleared.

After logout, any request using the same access token returns `401`.

Exchange an active refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair.

**Rotation:** The old refresh token is immediately revoked. Reusing an old (rotated) refresh token triggers full session revocation and returns `401 REFRESH_TOKEN_REUSED`.

The refresh token can be sent via cookie (auto) or in the request body.

Validate and decode a Bearer token. Used by other microservices to verify identity without owning the JWT secret.

**200:** `{ valid: true, user: { id, tenantId, email, role } }`
**401:** `{ valid: false, error: 'TOKEN_EXPIRED' }` or similar

Confirm an email address using the token from the verification email.

Set `{{verifyToken}}` manually from the email link. On success the user's `emailVerified` flag is set to `true`.
Resend the email verification link. Rate limited to `OTP_RATE_LIMIT` per window.

Trigger a password-reset email.

**Email enumeration prevention:** Always returns `200` regardless of whether the email exists. This prevents attackers from discovering valid accounts.
Reset the password using the token from the reset email. The token is compared using timing-safe equality against the stored hash. All active sessions are revoked after a successful reset.

Verify a 6-digit OTP delivered by `email`, `sms`, or `totp`.

`purpose` values: `email_verification`, `phone_verification`, `login`, `sensitive_op`
Resend an OTP. Rate limited to prevent abuse.

item.name: Auth â€” Protected
item.description: Endpoints requiring a valid JWT Bearer token. The collection-level auth automatically injects `{{accessToken}}`.

request.description: Return the authenticated user's profile. The profile is scoped to `tenantId` extracted from the JWT â€” a user from `tenant-a` cannot access data from `tenant-b`.
Update allowed profile fields. Usernames are unique within a tenant. Attempting to set a username already taken by another user in the same tenant returns `409 Conflict`.
Change the current user's password. Verifies the current password first. On success, all active sessions are invalidated and a `PASSWORD_CHANGED` email is sent.
List all active sessions for the current user (includes device, IP, and creation time).
Revoke a specific session by `sessionId`. The associated refresh token is blacklisted.
Revoke all sessions except the current one. Useful for "sign out everywhere" functionality.
List all trusted devices recognized for the current user.
Remove a trusted device. Future logins from that device fingerprint will trigger a new-device notification.

Initialize TOTP setup. Returns a `secret` and `qrCodeUrl` for the user to scan with an authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.).

TOTP is NOT active until confirmed via `POST /mfa/totp/verify`.

exec: console.log('TOTP secret saved â€” scan the QR code with your authenticator app');

request.description: Confirm TOTP setup by submitting the current code from your authenticator app. On success, TOTP is activated and backup codes are generated.
Disable TOTP for the current account. Requires the current password as confirmation.
List the remaining TOTP backup codes. Each code is single-use.
Invalidate all existing backup codes and generate a new set.
Verify a phone number with the OTP received via SMS.

Self-service account unlock â€” step 1.

If the account is locked (too many failed login attempts), sends an `ACCOUNT_UNLOCK_REQUESTED` email with a one-time unlock link valid for **1 hour**.

Always returns `200` regardless of whether the email exists (no enumeration).

Self-service account unlock â€” step 2.

Submit the token received in the email to clear the lockout and re-enable login.

Set `{{unlockToken}}` to the token from the `ACCOUNT_UNLOCK_REQUESTED` email link.

Update the current user's OTP / MFA preferences.

All fields are optional â€” send only what you want to change.

| Field | Type | Options |
|---|---|---|
| `preferredMethod` | string | `totp` / `email` / `sms` |
| `requireForLogin` | boolean | Enforce OTP on every login |
| `requireForSensitiveOps` | boolean | Enforce OTP for password change, account deletion, etc. |

Mark or unmark a known device as trusted.

Set `{{deviceId}}` using the **Get Devices** response.

`trusted: true` â€” skips new-device email alerts on next login from this device.
`trusted: false` â€” revokes trust; next login from this device will trigger a new-device alert.

Returns the current user's security event log â€” sorted newest-first.

Includes events such as: `LOGIN_SUCCESS`, `LOGIN_FAILED`, `PASSWORD_CHANGED`, `MFA_ENABLED`, `ACCOUNT_LOCKED`, `SUSPICIOUS_LOGIN`, etc.
GDPR data portability â€” export all stored data for the current user as JSON.
GDPR right-to-erasure â€” anonymize the user's PII (email, name, phone replaced with anonymized values), revoke all tokens, and send a confirmation email. This is irreversible.

item.name: Social Auth
item.description: Social OAuth login and account linking. The frontend completes the OAuth flow and sends the provider token to this service.

request.description:
Authenticate via a social provider. The provider ID token/access token is validated against the provider's API.

**Supported providers:** `google`, `facebook`, `github`, `twitter`, `apple`, `linkedin`, `microsoft`, `discord`

**Behavior:**
- Existing user â†’ issue JWT
- New user â†’ create account + issue JWT
Link a new social provider to an existing account. JWT required.
Unlink a social provider from the current account. The user must have a password set (or another provider linked) before unlinking.
Return all social providers linked to the current account.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Admin-only endpoints. Requires a JWT with `role: admin` or `role: super_admin`. Set `{{adminAccessToken}}` (login as an admin user and manually copy the token, or use the Login request in a separate environment).

request.description: Manually unlock an account locked due to too many failed login attempts.

query.description: Filter sessions for a specific user

request.description: List all active sessions across all users for the current tenant.
Force-revoke any user's session by sessionId. Useful for incident response.
Paginated auth audit logs for the tenant. Filter by `event`, `userId`, date range.
Returns login success/fail counts, MFA adoption rate, new user registration trends, and top locked accounts for the tenant.

item.name: Security Tests
item.description: Requests to validate security controls. Run these to confirm the service correctly rejects malicious or invalid inputs.

request.description: Omits the required `X-Tenant-Id` header. Must return `400 Bad Request`.

exec: pm.test('Rejects with 400 when tenant header missing', () => {

request.description: Uses a tenant ID that fails the `^[a-z0-9_-]{3,64}$` pattern. Must return `400 Bad Request`.
Attempts NoSQL injection via `$gt` operator in the email field. `express-mongo-sanitize` strips operator keys; the validator then rejects the sanitized value.
Password fails strength requirements (min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, digit, special char). Must return `400`.
Submits a clearly invalid/expired JWT. Must return `401` with `{ valid: false, error: 'TOKEN_EXPIRED' }` or `INVALID_TOKEN`.

item.name: [SHOULD 401] No Bearer Token on Protected Route

request.description: Calls a protected endpoint with no Authorization header. Must return `401 Unauthorized`.
Uses the regular user's `{{accessToken}}` (not admin) to hit an admin-only route. Must return `403 Forbidden`.
Requests a password reset for an email that does not exist. Must return `200` (email enumeration prevention â€” never reveal whether the account exists).
